review_reason: Path A (Direct F Proof) proven impossible -- no formula psi exists with both 'derives psi' and 'neg(psi) in M'. The irreflexivity proof REQUIRES constructing a second MCS M'. Pivot to implementation-005.md (Path B) recommended. Two pre-existing sorries remain at CanonicalIrreflexivity.lean lines 1245 and 1328.

artifacts.description: Phase 1 closure lemmas (5 theorems, sorry-free) + Path A impossibility analysis
Path B plan skeleton with 4 candidate approaches
Implementation summary documenting Path A analysis and pivot

partial_progress.details: Path A (Direct F Proof) systematically explored and proven impossible. Phase 1 closure lemmas completed (5 sorry-free theorems). Phase 2 confirmed: no formula psi exists with both 'derives psi' and 'neg(psi) in M' -- this is a logical impossibility for MCSs. Path B plan created with 4 candidate approaches. Two pre-existing sorries in CanonicalIrreflexivity.lean remain.

completion_data.completion_summary: Proved Path A (Direct F Proof) impossible for canonicalR irreflexivity. Implemented 5 sorry-free closure lemmas formalizing CanonicalR(M,M) consequences. Documented systematic analysis of 5 candidate formula classes, all shown unable to produce a contradiction within a single MCS. Created Path B plan (implementation-005.md) with 4 alternative approaches.

errors.message: Path A (Direct F Proof) is provably impossible: any theorem psi is in MCS M, so neg(psi) cannot be in M. The contradiction mechanism requires a second MCS M'.
errors.recommendation: Pivot to implementation-005.md. Priority: Approach 4 (GContent-fresh p) > Approach 3 (GContent transfer bridge) > Approach 1 (Product Frame) > Approach 2 (Zanardo axioms)
